GemObjectPrimitives.brief: A collection of constants representing virtual number identifiers for gem objects used in the game.
GemObjectPrimitives.details: The GemObjectPrimitives group defines constant values for the virtual number identifiers associated with various gem objects, such as rubies and emeralds. These constants serve as unique identifiers that enable consistent referencing, creation, and interaction with gem items throughout the game's codebase. Organizing these identifiers within a dedicated group enhances code clarity, maintainability, and reduces the likelihood of errors when handling gem-related objects. This structure supports modular and scalable management of item identifiers in the system.
GemObjectPrimitives.notes: Constants are organized within the group to ensure easy maintenance and to promote a clear, centralized reference point for gem object identifiers.
GemObjectPrimitives.rationale: This group exists to provide a standardized and maintainable way to identify gem objects via their virtual numbers, facilitating reliable object management and interaction within the game's architecture. It helps separate object identification concerns from other game logic, promoting modularity and clarity.
GemObjectPrimitives.name: GemObjectPrimitives

OBJ_VNUM_GEM_RUBY.brief: The constant representing the virtual number identifier for a ruby gem object.
OBJ_VNUM_GEM_RUBY.details: This constant defines the unique virtual number (vnum) assigned to ruby gem objects within the game, facilitating their identification and management. It is initialized to 121, corresponding to the game's object catalog entry for rubies. The vnum is used extensively in object creation, spawning, and lookup functions to ensure consistent referencing of ruby gem instances throughout the codebase. By using this constant, the code maintains clarity and reduces errors related to hardcoded values.
OBJ_VNUM_GEM_RUBY.notes: The vnum value is stored as a constexpr to enable compile-time optimization and to prevent accidental modifications, ensuring reliable identification of ruby gem objects.
OBJ_VNUM_GEM_RUBY.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to reference ruby gem objects within the game's code, supporting consistent object instantiation and interaction logic related to gem items.
OBJ_VNUM_GEM_RUBY.name: OBJ_VNUM_GEM_RUBY

OBJ_VNUM_GEM_EMERALD.brief: The constant representing the virtual number identifier for emerald gem objects.
OBJ_VNUM_GEM_EMERALD.details: This constant defines the unique virtual number (VNUM) assigned to emerald gem objects within the game, serving as a key identifier for these items. It is used throughout the codebase to instantiate, reference, and manage emerald gems, ensuring consistency in object handling. The value 122 corresponds to the specific entry in the game's object catalog, linking to the emerald gem template used in various game mechanics such as spawning, item recognition, and interactions.
OBJ_VNUM_GEM_EMERALD.notes: The constant is stored as a compile-time constexpr, ensuring efficient and immutable reference throughout the application. Its value aligns with the game's object cataloging system, facilitating easy updates if the object catalog changes.
OBJ_VNUM_GEM_EMERALD.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable, and consistent way to identify emerald gem objects within the game's code, supporting reliable object creation and interaction logic.
OBJ_VNUM_GEM_EMERALD.name: OBJ_VNUM_GEM_EMERALD
